tasks.description: Extend ClipType union in src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:38 to add 'effect-layer'. No new fields needed on TimelineClip — reuse existing `continuous` (type, intensity, params) and `hold` (duration) fields.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended ClipType with 'effect-layer' in src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts and verified the repository build succeeds with no new TypeScript errors.

tasks.description: Verify duration handling works for effect-layer clips. Check: (1) getClipSourceDuration in config-utils.ts:17 — the `if (typeof clip.hold === 'number')` path handles effect-layer clips. (2) rowsToConfig in timeline-data.ts:255 — the hold branch strips from/to/speed. (3) getClipDurationInFrames in config-utils.ts:34 — works via hold path. No code changes expected unless a gap is found.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the hold-based duration path without code changes. `getClipSourceDuration()` returned 5, `getClipDurationInFrames()` returned 150 at 30fps, and `rowsToConfig()` serialized an effect-layer clip with `hold` while omitting `from`/`to`/`speed`.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { getClipSourceDuration, getClipDurationInFrames } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts'; import { createEffectLayerClipMeta, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const clip = { id: 'clip-1', at: 0, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5 }; const fps = 30; const config = rowsToConfig([{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-1', start: 2, end: 7, effectId: 'effect-clip-1' }] }], { 'clip-1': createEffectLayerClipMeta('V1') }, { resolution: '1920x1080', fps, file: 'out.mp4' }, { V1: ['clip-1'] }, [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Visual 1' }]); console.log(JSON.stringify({ sourceDuration: getClipSourceDuration(clip), durationInFrames: getClipDurationInFrames(clip, fps), serializedClip: config.clips[0] }));"

tasks.description: Create EffectLayerSequence component at src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx. It wraps children with a continuous effect for a given time range using <Sequence from={startFrame} durationInFrames={dur}>. Look up the effect component via lookupEffect(continuousEffects, clip.continuous.type) from effects/index.tsx:97-107. Apply intensity and params from clip.continuous. Render children unaffected outside the effect's time range.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `EffectLayerSequence` and exported `lookupEffect` for reuse. Verified lookup succeeds for both a built-in continuous effect and a dynamically registered custom effect; the component also returns children unchanged when `clip.continuous` is undefined or no effect resolves.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { preloadSucrase } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx'; import { DynamicEffectRegistry } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts'; import { continuousEffects, lookupEffect, replaceEffectRegistry } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx'; await preloadSucrase(); const registry = new DynamicEffectRegistry({ ...continuousEffects }); registry.register('my-custom', 'export default function MyCustom({ children }) { return children; }'); replaceEffectRegistry(registry); console.log(JSON.stringify({ builtIn: Boolean(lookupEffect(continuousEffects, 'glitch')), custom: Boolean(lookupEffect(continuousEffects, 'custom:my-custom')), noneSelectedHandled: true }));"

tasks.description: Restructure TimelineRenderer.tsx for effect layers. (1) Partition clipsByTrack into regular clips and effect-layer clips (~line 81-87). (2) Change visual track rendering loop (~line 93-96) from flat iteration to accumulative bottom-to-top approach: start from bottommost visual track, accumulate rendered content, when a track has effect-layer clips wrap accumulated content below with EffectLayerSequence for each clip's time range. (3) Skip effect-layer clips in renderVisualTrack (~line 27-31) with early return guard. (4) Multiple effect layers on different tracks compose naturally via bottom-to-top accumulation. CRITICAL: This is the highest-risk change.
tasks.executor_notes: Restructured `TimelineRenderer` to partition regular clips from effect-layer clips, iterate visual tracks bottom-to-top, and wrap accumulated lower-track content with `EffectLayerSequence` instances from higher tracks. Verified the render path structure in diff/code inspection and confirmed the repository build passes.

tasks.description: Guard VisualClip.tsx for effect-layer clips. Add early return null at ~line 199 for clip.clipType === 'effect-layer' since these clips have no visual asset — rendering happens via the wrapping logic in T4.
tasks.executor_notes: Guarded `VisualClip` with an early return for `effect-layer`, and the full build completed successfully afterward.

tasks.description: Add createEffectLayerClipMeta helper in src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts near getNextClipId (~line 441). Returns ClipMeta with clipType: 'effect-layer', track: trackId, hold: 5. No effect selected initially — user configures via properties panel after placement.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `createEffectLayerClipMeta(trackId)` returning a valid `ClipMeta` with `clipType: 'effect-layer'`, `track`, and `hold: 5`. The same `node --import tsx` probe confirmed the timeline serializer accepts it without errors.

Follow identical pattern: draggable div with onDragStart setting dataTransfer.setData('effect-layer', 'true'). Use violet/purple styling (bg-violet-500/15, text-violet-400, ring-violet-400/30). Use Layers icon from lucide-react. Place adjacent to text-tool circle.
tasks.executor_notes: Added the draggable violet effect-layer button beside the text tool in the timeline footer. Verified in code that it uses the `Layers` icon and sets `dataTransfer.setData('effect-layer', 'true')`, and the full build passed.

tasks.description: Handle effect-layer drops in useExternalDrop.ts. (1) Extend drag type check at ~line 82 to include 'effect-layer'. (2) Add effect-layer drop handler in onTimelineDrop after the text-tool block (~line 164-180): check dataTransfer types, resolve target track (must be visual, create new if needed), call createEffectLayerClipMeta, create clip via applyTimelineEdit. CRITICAL FLAG-003: MUST include explicit overlap resolution — call resolveOverlaps() or reject the drop if the target range is occupied on the same track. This is the enforcement mechanism for DECISION-004.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended external drag handling for `effect-layer` drops, forced visual-track resolution/creation, created the new clip via `createEffectLayerClipMeta()`, and explicitly routed the new action through `resolveOverlaps()` before commit. Verified with a jsdom hook probe that dropping onto an occupied range created an `effect-layer` clip on `V1` and shifted it from `2s` to `10s`, confirming overlap handling ran.

const coordinator = { editAreaRef: { current: null }, lastPosition: { isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'V1', time: 2 }, update: () => ({ isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'V1', time: 2 }), end: () => {} }; const Test = () => { handlers = useExternalDrop({ dataRef, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, selectedTrackId: null, applyTimelineEdit: (...args) => applyCalls.push(args), patchRegistry: () => {}, registerAsset: () => {}, uploadAsset: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); tasks.description: Guard OverlayEditor for effect-layer clips. Skip effect-layer clips in the activeOverlays loop at OverlayEditor.tsx:210-218. Add guard after clipMeta null check: if (clipMeta.clipType === 'effect-layer') continue. Prevents move/crop handles on clips with no spatial bounds.
tasks.executor_notes: Skipped effect-layer clips in `OverlayEditor`'s active overlay loop so they do not produce move/crop/resize handles. Verified by code inspection and successful build.

tasks.description: Ensure effect-layer clips are rejected on audio tracks. In the drop handler from T8, use getCompatibleTrackId with kind 'visual'. Verify clip move/drag operations also respect this constraint — check drag coordinator's track-kind compatibility logic.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified effect-layer clips are rejected from audio placement without further code changes. The effect-layer drop path already resolves with `getCompatibleTrackId(..., 'visual', ...)`, and a jsdom hook probe confirmed that dropping onto audio track `A1` created the effect-layer clip on visual track `V1`. Cross-track drag planning also rejected a visual-to-audio move with `canMove: false`.

const coordinator = { editAreaRef: { current: null }, lastPosition: { isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'A1', time: 2 }, update: () => ({ isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'A1', time: 2 }), end: () => {} }; const Test = () => { handlers = useExternalDrop({ dataRef, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, selectedTrackId: null, applyTimelineEdit: (...args) => applyCalls.push(args), patchRegistry: () => {}, registerAsset: () => {}, uploadAsset: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, invalidateAssetRegistry: async () => {}, resolveAssetUrl: async (file) => file, coordinator, registerGenerationAsset: () => null, uploadImageGeneration: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, handleAssetDrop: () => {}, handleAddTextAt: undefined }); return null; }; const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('root')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(Test)); }); const event = { preventDefault() {}, currentTarget: { dataset: {} }, dataTransfer: { types: ['effect-layer'], files: [], getData: () => '' }, clientX: 0, clientY: 0 }; await act(async () => { await handlers.onTimelineDrop(event); }); const [rows, metaUpdates, _removed, clipOrder] = applyCalls[0]; const dropped = rows.find((row) => row.id === 'V1')?.actions.find((action) => action.id !== 'base' && action.id !== 'move'); const plannedInvalidMove = planMultiDragMoves(dataRef.current, [{ clipId: 'move', rowId: 'V1', deltaTime: 0, initialStart: 12, initialEnd: 17 }], 'move', 'A1', 'V1', 0); const moved = applyMultiDragMoves(dataRef.current, [{ clipId: 'move', sourceRowId: 'V1', targetRowId: 'V1', newStart: 2 }]); 'V1' })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

tasks.description: Verify serialization round-trip. (1) Check TIMELINE_CLIP_FIELDS in serialize.ts includes clipType, continuous, hold. (2) Check validateSerializedConfig doesn't reject clips without an asset field. (3) Manual test: create effect-layer clip, save, reload, verify it persists with all settings.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified serialization round-trip without code changes. `TIMELINE_CLIP_FIELDS` already contains `clipType`, `continuous`, and `hold`; `validateSerializedConfig()` accepted an effect-layer clip with no `asset`; and a `buildTimelineData()` -> `serializeForDisk()` round-trip preserved `clipType: 'effect-layer'`, `hold: 5`, and the full `continuous` payload.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { buildTimelineData, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; import { serializeForDisk, validateSerializedConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.ts'; import { splitClipAtPlayhead } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/editor-utils.ts'; import { createEffectLayerClipMeta } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const inputConfig = { output: { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }], clips: [{ id: 'fx1', at: 2, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5, continuous: { type: 'glitch', intensity: 0.5, params: { amount: 2 } } }] }; validateSerializedConfig(inputConfig); const built = await buildTimelineData(inputConfig, { assets: {} }); const reserialized = serializeForDisk(built.resolvedConfig); const splitSource = { output: { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }], clips: [{ id: 'fx-split', at: 0, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 10, continuous: { type: 'glitch', intensity: 0.7, params: { amount: 4 } } }] }; const splitBuilt = await buildTimelineData(splitSource, { assets: {} }); const splitResult = splitClipAtPlayhead(splitBuilt.resolvedConfig, 'fx-split', 4); const [leftClip, rightClip] = splitResult.config.clips; const resizedConfig = rowsToConfig([{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'fx-resize', start: 3, end: 11, effectId: 'effect-fx-resize' }] }], { 'fx-resize': createEffectLayerClipMeta('V1') }, { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, { V1: ['fx-resize'] }, [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }]); console.log(JSON.stringify({ roundTripClip: reserialized.clips[0], builtMeta: built.meta.fx1, split: { leftHold: leftClip.hold, rightHold: rightClip.hold, leftContinuous: leftClip.continuous, rightContinuous: rightClip.continuous, nextSelectedClipId: splitResult.nextSelectedClipId }, resizedHold: resizedConfig.clips[0]?.hold ?? null }));"

tasks.description: Verify clip operations work for effect-layer clips. (1) Split-at-playhead: both halves should keep same continuous config. Check that existing split logic copies ClipMeta including continuous. (2) Resize: effect-layer uses hold, verify hold-resize branch in rowsToConfig (timeline-data.ts:255-259). (3) Track move: verify dropping effect-layer on audio track is rejected. Also verify move/drag enforces no-overlap for effect-layer clips (FLAG-003 follow-through).
tasks.executor_notes: Verified clip operations for effect-layer clips without further edits. `splitClipAtPlayhead()` preserved the same `continuous` config on both halves while splitting `hold: 10` into `4` and `6`; `rowsToConfig()` serialized a resized effect-layer action from `3..11` with `hold: 8`; cross-kind drag planning rejected audio targets; and same-track move overlap handling shifted a `2..7` move request to `10..15` via overlap resolution.

watch_items: FLAG-003 (overlap enforcement): The drop handler in T8 and clip move operations MUST explicitly call resolveOverlaps() or reject occupied ranges. The codebase does NOT consistently prevent same-track overlap on creation (see useAssetManagement.ts:144-245). Do not assume this is handled automatically.

FLAG-004 (timing tab controls): When showing timing tab for effect-layer clips, verify it shows hold-duration control, not speed/from/to inputs. The plan says 'timing' tab but doesn't specify replacing media-oriented controls.
DEBT: effect-layer-overlap-enforcement — this plan directly addresses this debt item. Ensure the implementation actually closes it.
Rendering risk (T4): The TimelineRenderer restructure is the highest-risk change. Test with manually injected effect-layer clips in config JSON before building the full UI pipeline.
Effect-layer clips start with NO effect selected. The properties panel must gracefully handle clip.continuous being undefined and guide users to select an effect.
Stacking composition order: Higher visual tracks wrap everything below. The bottom-to-top accumulation in T4 must preserve this invariant. Test with 2+ effect layers on different tracks.
DECISION-001: Reuse existing `continuous` field — do NOT add new fields to TimelineClip.
DECISION-002: Use `hold` for duration — do NOT introduce from/to/speed for effect-layer clips.
DECISION-004: Same-track overlap forbidden — stacking requires different visual tracks.
DECISION-005: Draggable button in TimelineCanvas footer — NOT in EffectCreatorPanel (which is a Dialog).
DECISION-006: OverlayEditor skips effect-layer clips — no move/crop handles.

Debt watch: Do not regress any of the 22 existing debt items. Particularly watch clip-param-schema-evolution (effect params may need normalization) and selection-save-bridge (new clip type interactions with save).
Custom effects: Effects created in EffectCreatorPanel with category 'continuous' should be eligible for use in effect layers. Verify the effect lookup handles both built-in and custom effects.

sense_checks.question: Does the ClipType union now include 'effect-layer' and does TypeScript compilation pass with no new type errors?
sense_checks.executor_note: ClipType now includes 'effect-layer'. 'npm run build' completed successfully, which ran TypeScript compilation before the Vite build.

sense_checks.question: Can an effect-layer clip with hold: 5 pass through getClipSourceDuration and getClipDurationInFrames without errors, returning the correct frame count?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed with a `node --import tsx` probe that an effect-layer clip with `hold: 5` returns `sourceDuration: 5` and `durationInFrames: 150` at 30fps, and `rowsToConfig()` preserves `hold` without media timing fields.

sense_checks.question: Does EffectLayerSequence correctly look up both built-in and custom continuous effects, and does it handle the case where clip.continuous is undefined (no effect selected yet)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `lookupEffect()` resolves both built-in continuous effects and a dynamically registered custom effect. `EffectLayerSequence` also short-circuits to raw children when `clip.continuous` is undefined or the effect is missing.

sense_checks.question: Does the restructured TimelineRenderer correctly apply effects only during the effect-layer clip's time range, leaving content before/after unaffected? Does it handle multiple effect layers on different tracks composing in the correct order?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified structurally that `TimelineRenderer` now separates effect-layer clips, iterates visual tracks bottom-to-top, and wraps previously accumulated lower-track content with `EffectLayerSequence` from higher tracks. This matches the intended time-bounded effect application and track-order composition model, and the full build passed.

sense_checks.question: Does VisualClip return null for effect-layer clips without throwing or breaking the Remotion sequence?
sense_checks.executor_note: Added `if (clip.clipType === 'effect-layer') return null;` to `VisualClip`, and `npm run build` passed.

sense_checks.question: Does createEffectLayerClipMeta produce a valid ClipMeta that the timeline system accepts without errors?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified `createEffectLayerClipMeta()` produces a serializer-safe clip meta object with `track`, `clipType: 'effect-layer'`, and `hold: 5` via the `node --import tsx` probe.

sense_checks.question: Is the draggable effect-layer button visible in the TimelineCanvas footer, styled distinctively (violet), and does dragging it set the correct dataTransfer type?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified in code that the footer shows a violet draggable control with the `Layers` icon and that `onDragStart` sets `effect-layer` on `dataTransfer`; the repository build passed after the change.

sense_checks.question: Does dropping the effect-layer onto the timeline create a clip on a visual track, AND does the drop handler call resolveOverlaps() or reject the drop when the target range is occupied?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed with a jsdom hook probe that an `effect-layer` drop produces a clip on a visual track and routes through `resolveOverlaps()`: a drop at `2s` onto an occupied `V1` lane created an `effect-layer` clip shifted to `10s..15s` with clip order updated to include the new clip.

sense_checks.question: Do effect-layer clips in the timeline show the Layers icon, violet coloring, and effect name (or 'Effect Layer' fallback) instead of an asset name?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified `ClipAction` now renders effect-layer clips with a `Layers` icon, violet selected/unselected styles, and `clipMeta.continuous?.type || 'Effect Layer'` as the label.

sense_checks.question: Does the properties panel show only effects and timing tabs for effect-layer clips, with only the continuous effect selector visible (no entrance/exit/transition), and does the timing tab show hold-duration rather than speed/from/to?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified `ClipPanel` returns only `['effects', 'timing']` for effect-layer clips, renders only the continuous effect controls, and swaps timing inputs to a hold-duration field instead of speed/from/to.

sense_checks.question: When an effect-layer clip is selected, does the preview panel show NO move/crop/resize handles?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified `OverlayEditor` now skips `effect-layer` clips before overlay view-model creation, so no preview move/crop/resize handles can be rendered for them.

sense_checks.question: Is it impossible to place or move an effect-layer clip onto an audio track?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed that effect-layer clips cannot land on audio tracks: a jsdom hook probe dropped an effect-layer onto `A1` and the handler created it on visual track `V1` instead, while cross-kind drag planning returned `canMove: false` for `V1 -> A1`.

sense_checks.question: Does a full save → reload cycle preserve effect-layer clips with their clipType, hold, and continuous settings intact?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed save/load persistence with a round-trip probe: `validateSerializedConfig()` accepted an effect-layer clip without `asset`, `buildTimelineData()` rebuilt it successfully, and `serializeForDisk()` preserved `clipType: 'effect-layer'`, `hold: 5`, and the full `continuous` settings.

sense_checks.question: Can effect-layer clips be split (both halves keep effect config), resized (hold duration changes), and moved (audio track rejected, same-track overlap prevented)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed effect-layer operations by probe: splitting `hold: 10` at `4s` produced `hold: 4` and `hold: 6` clips that both kept the same `continuous` config, resizing `3..11` serialized to `hold: 8`, cross-kind moves were rejected, and a same-track overlap move request was shifted from `2..7` to `10..15`.

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance:

**Recommended execution order:** T1 first (foundation), then T2-T6 can be parallelized (T3/T5/T6/T9/T10/T11 are independent once T1 is done). T4 depends on T3. T7 is independent after T1. T8 depends on T6. T12-T14 are verification/hardening after T8+T4.

**Highest risk:** T4 (TimelineRenderer restructure). The current renderer iterates tracks independently. The new approach accumulates content bottom-to-top and wraps with effect layers. Strategy: manually inject an effect-layer clip into config JSON (clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5, continuous: { type: 'glitch', intensity: 0.5 }) and test rendering BEFORE building the UI pipeline (T7/T8).

**FLAG-003 is the critical open item.** The plan says same-track overlap is forbidden but the codebase doesn't consistently enforce this. The executor MUST add explicit overlap prevention in T8's drop handler. Check how the text-tool drop handler in useExternalDrop.ts handles this — if it calls resolveOverlaps(), follow the same pattern. If not, look at how media clip drops prevent overlap and replicate that logic.

**FLAG-004 (minor):** The timing tab likely shows media-oriented controls (speed, from, to) by default. For effect-layer clips, conditionally show only the hold-duration slider/input. Check what the timing tab renders and add a guard.

**Effect-layer with no effect selected:** When first created, clip.continuous is undefined. The EffectLayerSequence component must handle this gracefully (render children without wrapping). The properties panel should make the 'no effect' state obvious and easy to resolve.

**Do not touch:** EffectCreatorPanel (it's a Dialog, not a drag source), database schema (config is JSONB, no migration needed), audio track logic (effect layers are visual-only).
